rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope; do not introduce new architecture.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions: numeric variables must have numeric expressions, Boolean variables must have Boolean expressions.
Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type scopes; move valid bindings to implementation annexes.
Delete invalid AGREE clauses that reference undefined or unresolvable identifiers instead of attempting to define them.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only identifiers visible in the current scope to avoid resolution errors.
Type mismatches in AGREE expressions should be resolved by aligning helper declarations with their actual expression types.
Assign statements in implementation scope must target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Validator diagnostics pointing to invalid references should be addressed by removing or rewriting clauses, not by adding new architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must maintain type consistency; e.g., int variables require int expressions.
Use only declared constants, equations, or visible component features in AGREE annexes.
Implementation-level bindings belong in implementation scopes, not component type annexes.
AGREE annexes use specific syntax: assume, guarantee, eq, const, and assign statements.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to define or resolve missing identifiers by introducing new architecture or package-qualified names.
Avoid placing implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes; this causes scope resolution errors.
Do not assume that AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) are automatically available as AGREE variables.
Do not generate AGREE expressions with mismatched types unless the model explicitly defines compatible types.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs. implementations
Model Analyst's role in extracting AADL structure
Fusion of model components and owners
